title: Confirm or amend M1 epic dependency graph against both verdicts
description:
**Why.** M0 is the entry gate to M1. Without an explicit readiness statement, M1 work could begin against an unamended plan.

**Scope boundary.** M1 plan only. ADR/schema/NFR/OQ updates are .10–.13.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Why before M1; §Epic m0.03.5.
acceptance_criteria:
green / yellow on both spikes → M1 plan unchanged; confirmation noted in M0 close commit
red on either spike → m1.03 amended (HNSW pivot ADR or override-schema ADR) before M1 work resumes
M0 close commit links to both spike commit hashes and to all updated ADRs/docs (ADR-S0001, SOLO-08, SOLO-13, OQ-S001/S003/S006)
M1 readiness statement committed alongside M0 close

title: Update SOLO-08 §3.1 / §3.2 / §4 per branch-in-PK verdict
description:
**Why.** SOLO-08 §3 schema is the substrate M1's m1.03 builds on; a red verdict requires the schema to be rewritten before m1.03 begins.

**Scope boundary.** SOLO-08 sections only (and a delta-override ADR if red). ADR-S0001 is .10; SOLO-13 is .12.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Outcomes branch-in-PK table; §Epic m0.03.2.
acceptance_criteria:
green / yellow → SOLO-08 §3 / §4 unchanged; a note records the curve and the m1.08 re-verification expectation
red → an ADR for the delta-override schema ("Option C") is drafted and accepted; SOLO-08 §3.1 / §3.2 are rewritten against it before M1's m1.03 begins
SOLO-08 commit cites the branch-pk spike commit hash from .9

title: Update ADR-S0001 status per vector-substrate verdict
description:
**Why.** ADR-S0001 names sqlite-vec as the entire vector substrate; its status must reflect measured numbers before M1's m1.03 begins.

**Scope boundary.** ADR-S0001 only (and a replacement/pivot ADR if required by verdict). SOLO-08 is .11; SOLO-13 is .12.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Outcomes vector table; §Epic m0.03.1.
acceptance_criteria:
green → status remains accepted; new Measured section added with numbers from .5
yellow → status remains accepted; OQ-S003 gains a numeric kill criterion derived from the curve
red-quality → ADR-S0001 amended or retracted; replacement ADR drafted and accepted citing measured failure data
red-ceiling → ADR-S0001 amended citing the ceiling number; OQ-S003 HNSW pivot ADR promoted from M3 to M1 work and accepted before m1.03 begins
ADR commit cites the vector spike commit hash from .5

title: M0 — Substrate Spike (sqlite-vec + branch-in-PK)
description:
# M0 — Substrate Spike

Canonical doc: `docs/milestones/M0.md` (extract below).

## Problem
ADR-S0001 names sqlite-vec as the entire vector substrate, and SOLO-08 §4 puts `branch` in the PK of nodes/edges/findings. Both rest on measurements that have not been taken. Building M1 on a failing assumption forces a fallback design under schedule pressure with code already written on top.

## Approach
Two throwaway Go spike harnesses against the target schemas — one for sqlite-vec at 50k/1M vectors, one for branch-in-PK at 50 branches × 100k symbols. Numbers feed decision matrices in M0.md §Outcomes. Failures amend the relevant ADR/schema before M1 starts.

## Goals
- Vector-substrate verdict (green/yellow/red-quality/red-ceiling) with warm p95, recall@10, vec0 ceiling at 50k and 1M
- Branch-in-PK verdict with row growth, disk size, indexed-lookup p95, GC sweep cost
- ADR-S0001, SOLO-08, SOLO-13, OQ-S001/S003/S006 updated against measured numbers
- M1 plan confirmed or amended before m1.03 begins

## Out of scope
- Any production code in `internal/`
- Parser, promotion pipeline, application layer
- Real-repo data (m1.08 re-verifies later)
- All spike code is throwaway; deleted or moved to attic when M0 closes

## Constraints
- Reference hardware: 8-core, 16 GiB RAM, NVMe SSD (M1 MacBook Air or equivalent x86-64 laptop). Numbers measured on this tier are normative.
- Vec0 ceiling must be ≥ 250 000 embedded nodes to avoid red-ceiling verdict.

## Risks / open questions
- OQ-S001 (vec0 latency/recall envelope), OQ-S003 (HNSW pivot trigger), OQ-S006 (branch-in-PK scaling) all resolve out of this milestone.
- A red verdict on either spike blocks M1's m1.03; m0.03 owns the amendment work.

See `docs/milestones/M0.md` for the full outcome tables, sub-issue checklist, and rationale.

title: Resolve OQ-S001 and OQ-S006; tighten OQ-S003 if yellow
description:
**Why.** OQ-S001 and OQ-S006 are the open questions M0 exists to close; OQ-S003's numeric kill criterion is needed for the M3 gate to be meaningful.

**Scope boundary.** Open-question docs only.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Outcomes (both tables); §Epic m0.03.4.
acceptance_criteria:
OQ-S001 marked resolved with measured numbers from .5
OQ-S006 marked resolved with measured numbers from .9
If vector verdict is yellow, OQ-S003 carries a numeric kill criterion derived from the actual curve, gated at M3

title: Replace SOLO-13 §3 BUDGET (unmeasured) rows with BUDGET (measured M0)
description:
**Why.** SOLO-13 §3 currently carries placeholder budgets; the M0 close commit must leave no unmeasured row in the vector-path or branch-in-PK sections.

**Scope boundary.** SOLO-13 §3 row labels and numbers only.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Exit gates final paragraph; §Epic m0.03.3.
acceptance_criteria:
Vector-path rows updated with warm p95, recall, ceiling from .5
Branch-in-PK rows updated with indexed-lookup p95, disk, GC numbers from .9
Each updated row cites the originating spike commit hash
SOLO-13 §3 has no BUDGET (unmeasured) rows for vector-path or branch-in-PK

title: Write tools/loadtest/spikes/branchpk/RESULTS.md with verdict
description:
**Why.** RESULTS.md is the artifact m0.03 cites for SOLO-08 amendments; m1.08 will compare its real-repo numbers against the assumptions recorded here.

**Scope boundary.** Reporting only. SOLO-08/SOLO-13 amendments are .11/.12.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.02.4; §Outcomes branch-in-PK table.
acceptance_criteria:
Report cites row growth, disk size, indexed-lookup p95, GC sweep cost
Outcome bucket assigned (green / yellow / red) per the M0 §Outcomes branch-in-PK table; verdict cell quotes the matching action text
SQLite version, host platform string, and the synthetic-population assumptions (overlap %, finding density) cited so m1.08 can compare
RESULTS.md committed under tools/loadtest/spikes/branchpk/

title: Bench branch-GC sweep wall-clock and disk reclaim
description:
**Why.** Whether branch-GC keeps the steady state bounded is the difference between a green and a red branch-in-PK verdict.

**Scope boundary.** GC sweep against a synthetic 10-of-50 deletion. No real-repo data. No production gc command.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Branch-in-PK exit-gate bullet 4; §Epic m0.02.3.
acceptance_criteria:
10-of-50 branches deleted via DELETE FROM nodes WHERE branch NOT IN (...) plus matching edges and findings cascades
Wall-clock time and disk-reclaim bytes recorded
WAL size before and after recorded
Results emitted as JSON

title: Bench eng_get_node and eng_get_edges indexed-lookup p95
description:
**Why.** Indexed-lookup p95 is the primary input to the branch-in-PK decision matrix and to SOLO-13 §3.1 budgets.

**Scope boundary.** Indexed lookups only. GC sweep is .8.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Branch-in-PK exit-gate bullet 3; §Epic m0.02.2.
acceptance_criteria:
Warm p50/p95/p99 recorded for SELECT * FROM nodes WHERE node_id=? AND branch=?
Warm p50/p95/p99 recorded for SELECT * FROM edges WHERE src_node_id=? AND branch=? AND kind=?
Numbers compared against SOLO-13 §3.1 budgets in the JSON output

title: Synthetic loader for nodes/edges/findings against SOLO-08 §3 schema
description:
**Why.** Establishes the multi-branch population that the indexed-lookup bench (.7) and GC bench (.8) run against. Direct INSERT loops only — measurement is about row-count and storage cost, not parser fidelity.

**Scope boundary.** Loading only. No bench, no GC, no real-repo data.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.02.1; §Branch-in-PK schema exit-gate bullets 1–2.
acceptance_criteria:
Program in tools/loadtest/spikes/branchpk/ loads 100k symbols × 50 branches with configurable per-branch dirty overlap (10%, 30%, 60%)
Parallel findings population at 1 finding per 100 symbols per branch (per SOLO-04 §8.1's branch-in-PK rule)
Schema reproduced verbatim from SOLO-08 §3.1/§3.2 (or vendored from internal/infrastructure/sqlite/migrations/ if available)
engram.db produced at all three overlap settings; on-disk size, WAL size, RSS recorded as JSON

title: Write tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/RESULTS.md with verdict
description:
**Why.** RESULTS.md is the artifact m0.03 cites; without a single committed report the M0 close commit has no anchor.

**Scope boundary.** Reporting only. ADR/SOLO-08/SOLO-13 amendments are .10/.11/.12.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.5; §Outcomes vector table.
acceptance_criteria:
Report cites all four exit-gate numbers (warm p95 at 50k+1M, recall at 50k+1M, vec0 ceiling, RSS/disk)
Outcome bucket assigned (green / yellow / red-quality / red-ceiling) per the M0 §Outcomes vector table; verdict cell quotes the matching action text
sqlite-vec version, SQLite version, and host platform string cited
RESULTS.md committed under tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/

title: Compute recall@10 and recall@50 against a 100-pair hold-out
description:
**Why.** The recall@10 ≥ 0.95 floor at 50k is the working number that gates a green vector verdict; the curve is OQ-S001's resolution.

**Scope boundary.** Hold-out generation and recall computation only. Latency is .3.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Vector substrate exit-gate bullet 3; §Epic m0.01.4.
acceptance_criteria:
Hold-out generation deterministic from a seed; 100 pairs at each population
recall@10 and recall@50 numbers reported at 50k and 1M
Results emitted as JSON consumable by the report task (.5)

title: Bench warm/cold k=10 and k=50 query latency at 50k and 1M
description:
**Why.** Warm p95 and the vec0 ceiling are the primary inputs to the M0 §Outcomes vector decision matrix and to SOLO-13 §3.3.1's gate.

**Scope boundary.** Latency only. Recall is .4. No correctness check beyond returning k results.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Vector substrate exit-gate bullets 1, 2, 5; §Epic m0.01.3.
acceptance_criteria:
p50/p95/p99/max recorded warm and cold (post-restart) for both 50k and 1M populations at k=10 and k=50
Vec0 ceiling determined by sweeping populations 50k–2M to find the crossover of the SOLO-13 §3.1 semantic_search p95 budget OR the §3.3 soft RSS cap (smaller of the two crossovers wins)
Results emitted as JSON with sqlite-vec version, SQLite version, and host platform string
close_reason: DoD verified: warm/cold latency bench + ceiling sweep, 8 passing race-clean tests

title: Load synthetic vectors into vec0 virtual table at 50k and 1M
description:
**Why.** Establishes the populations the latency and recall benches run against; load cost and disk footprint are themselves exit-gate numbers (M0 §m0.01 DoD bullet 3).

**Scope boundary.** Load loop only. Query bench is .3, recall is .4. Reads vectors produced by .1.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.2.

**Clarifications (proceed confirmed):**
- Go SQLite driver: github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec-go-bindings (official sqlite-vec Go bindings, CGo, bundles the extension).
- vec_nodes schema: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_nodes USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[768])
- Loader runs two passes: 50k vectors, then 1M vectors (generate 1M with vecbin, or re-use 50k file loaded 20x — prefer generating 1M directly).
- Metrics JSON shape per population: {"population": 50000, "load_wall_ms": 1234, "disk_bytes": 56789, "peak_rss_bytes": 98765}
- Output: tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/data/load_metrics.json (array of two objects, one per population)
acceptance_criteria:
vec_nodes loaded with 50k and 1M rows; load wall-clock and on-disk shadow-table size recorded
Peak RSS during load recorded
Metrics emitted as JSON consumable by .3, .4, .5
close_reason: DoD verified: vec0 loader with RSS + disk metrics, 7 passing race-clean tests

title: Generate synthetic 768-dim vectors with realistic L2 norms
description:
**Why.** The vec0 spike (M0 §m0.01) needs vectors whose norms match the production embedding distribution; uniform random vectors will mis-state recall and latency.

**Scope boundary.** Vector generation only. Loading into vec0 is .2; benching is .3; recall is .4. No daemon, no internal/ code.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.1.

**Clarifications (proceed confirmed):**
- Disk format: flat binary — uint64 count + raw float32 dims per vector (no per-vector dim prefix). Both gen and loader use this same struct.
- Norm distribution: Gaussian(μ=7.5, σ=1.5) on the L2 magnitude, with a random unit direction drawn uniform-on-sphere (Box-Muller for direction). This approximates nomic-embed-text without requiring the model.
- Output file: tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/data/vectors.bin (created by gen, consumed by loader in .2).
acceptance_criteria:
Go program in tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/ emits N vectors drawn from the empirical norm distribution of nomic-embed-text on a corpus snippet
Distribution sample (mean, p50, p95 norm) printed to stdout
Vectors written to disk in a format the loader (.2) consumes
close_reason: DoD verified: vecbin format + gen package with realistic norms, 7 passing race-clean tests
